title: CatBoost: Unbiased boosting with categorical features
methodology: Theoretical proof of the bias-elimination property + benchmark on multiple datasets including KDD'09 Appetency, Internet, Adult.
contributions: Naive target encoding induces 'prediction shift' (a target-leakage-like bias) when used inside gradient boosting; ordered target statistics eliminate this by training each instance only on predecessors in a random permutation.
Ordered boosting extends the same principle to the gradient estimates themselves.
weaknesses: Slower than LightGBM out of the box; ordering overhead can dominate on very large data.
The 'unbiased' guarantee is asymptotic; finite-sample behaviour can still leak signal.
emerald_relevance: Best-in-class GBDT for high-cardinality categoricals (Industry ~30 levels, Borrower State ~50 levels in our dataset). Strong candidate to win the GBDT family comparison; included in section 5.8 panel.
notes: Ordered boosting eliminates target-leakage in target encoding. Best-in-class for high-cardinality categoricals.
